basics.name: Tingyu Zhang
basics.label: Master's Student in Computer Science | ML Research & Engineering
basics.summary: Master's student in Computer Science at Northeastern University Seattle (4.0 GPA) with a background in Applied Mathematics. Published researcher at ICLR 2026, with hands-on experience in ML systems, LLM agents, and production engineering.

work.name: Southern University of Science and Technology
highlights: Designed and trained a continual learning Multi-Armed Bandit (MAB) model with parallel optimization, formulating a preference-theoretic reward function analogous to RLHF feedback modeling; implemented training loop in PyTorch and TensorFlow with vectorized batch updates to improve convergence efficiency.
Modeled the influence maximization problem as an online combinatorial MAB over a stochastic graph.

work.name: HAOCHENG TECH HOLDING
highlights: Engineered end-to-end ML training pipeline integrating Random Forest and Logistic Regression for material classification on large-scale tabular data, achieving 87.3% accuracy; applied TF-IDF vectorization and NLP preprocessing via NLTK and scikit-learn.
Designed PostgreSQL schema with B-tree and composite indexes on high-cardinality video metadata fields, achieving a 15× latency reduction (30 min → 2 min).

education.institution: Southern University of Science and Technology

projects.name: AI-Powered Job Search Agent with Ollama
projects.summary: Developed autonomous job search agent using Ollama with Llama 3.2 model running locally, implementing RAG architecture with ChromaDB vector store for personalized job matching.
highlights: Engineered multi-agent system for resume tailoring, cover letter generation, and application tracking using LangChain and Python, reducing application preparation time from 2 hours to 15 minutes per job.
Built web scraping pipeline using Selenium and BeautifulSoup to aggregate job postings, integrated with SQLite database and Celery task queue, collecting 2K+ daily job listings.

projects.name: Astronomical Image Classifier: ViT Fine-Tuning & LLM Agent
projects.summary: Fine-tuned a Vision Transformer (ViT) for multi-class astronomical image classification using LoRA on a remote GPU server via SSH, achieving 90.4% accuracy while reducing trainable parameters by over 90%.
highlights: Built a supervised few-shot learning corpus of 500+ prompt-completion pairs with interpolation curve ground-truth labels via a prompt engineering pipeline.
Benchmarked against LeNet-5 trained on MNIST (60K images, 80.3% accuracy) as baseline.

projects.name: Cloud-Native Microservices for Distributed System
projects.summary: Architected cloud-native microservices on AWS ECS with Docker containerization, Application Load Balancer for traffic routing, and auto-scaling policies.
highlights: Integrated RabbitMQ message queues with Apache Commons Pool for connection pooling, implementing manual acknowledgements and retry logic to ensure reliable asynchronous processing.

projects.name: SpringBoot FullStack Book Management Web Application
projects.summary: Architected full-stack application using React 19 with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS for the frontend, Spring Boot with PostgreSQL for the backend.
highlights: Developed RESTful APIs with Spring MVC supporting full CRUD operations, including multipart file uploads for book covers with client-side image preview using the FileReader API.
